target.title: #92589: fix(internal-runtime-context): wrap prompt-preface runtime context body in delimiters
target.body:
## Summary

Wrap the runtime context body in `buildRuntimeContextMessageContent` with `INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_BEGIN` / `END` delimiters so `stripInternalRuntimeContext` can fully remove leaked runtime context from user-visible surfaces.

## Root Cause

`buildRuntimeContextMessageContent` (in `runtime-context-prompt.ts`) constructs hidden runtime context prompt blocks using the "prompt preface" format:

```
OpenClaw runtime context for the immediately preceding user message.
This context is runtime-generated, not user-authored. Keep internal details private.

<runtime context body — relevant-memories, sender metadata, etc.>
```

When the model echoes this context back in its reply, `stripInternalRuntimeContext` strips the header and notice lines via `stripRuntimeContextPromptPreface`, but the **body** (containing sensitive data like `<relevant-memories>`, `Sender (untrusted metadata)`, `Conversation info`) was not wrapped in the delimiters that `stripDelimitedBlock` handles. The body leaked through into channel card rendering (Feishu streaming cards).

**Fix**: Wrap the runtime context body in `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` / `<<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` delimiters (3 lines added). `stripInternalRuntimeContext` already calls `stripDelimitedBlock` first (which handles nested delimiters correctly), then `stripLegacyInternalRuntimeContext`, then `stripRuntimeContextPromptPreface`. With the body now delimited, the entire context block is removed regardless of escape path.

## Real behavior proof

### behavior

When a runtime context block (containing `<relevant-memories>`, `Sender (untrusted metadata)`, `Conversation info`, system timestamps) is echoed in a reply, `stripInternalRuntimeContext` now completely removes the entire block instead of leaving the context body visible.

### environment

- OS: Linux x64, kernel 4.19.112-2.el8
- Runtime: Node.js v24.13.1, tsx v4.22.3
- openclaw: fix/issue-92589-* branch at a0f80950ed on top of origin/main (6cf06e8e7e)

### steps

1. Checkout `fix/issue-92589-*` branch in openclaw workspace
2. Build runtime context with sensitive metadata via production `buildRuntimeContextSystemContext`:
   ```
   npx tsx scripts/repro-92589.mjs
   ```
3. Verify `stripInternalRuntimeContext` removes all sensitive data from a simulated leaked reply

### Evidence after fix

**Reproduction script — real production code execution via tsx:**

```
========================================================================
Real behavior proof — #92589
fix: wrap runtime context body in internal context delimiters
========================================================================

── Scenario: runtime context contains sensitive metadata ──

Built runtime context (delimiters now wrap the body):
---
OpenClaw runtime context for the immediately preceding user message.
This context is runtime-generated, not user-authored. Keep internal details private.

<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>
Sender (untrusted metadata):
```json
{"open_id": "ou_abc123", "name": "Alice"}
```

Conversation info (untrusted metadata):
```json
{"chat_id": "oc_xyz789", "message_id": "om_456"}
```

<relevant-memories>
Memory: Bob uses Python for backend work.
Memory: Carol prefers dark mode.
</relevant-memories>

System: [2026-06-13 11:34:26 GMT+8] Feishu[default] DM | ou_xxx
<<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>
---

Contains BEGIN delimiter: ✅ YES
Contains END delimiter:   ✅ YES

── Simulated leaked reply (context mixed with actual reply) ──

After stripInternalRuntimeContext:
---
Here is my analysis of the data:

Would you like me to proceed further?
---

Stripped contains <relevant-memories>:     ✅ NO (clean)
Stripped contains Sender metadata:        ✅ NO (clean)
Stripped contains Conversation metadata:  ✅ NO (clean)
Stripped contains user open_id:           ✅ NO (clean)
Stripped contains context header:         ✅ NO (clean)

Actual reply text preserved:              ✅ YES

========================================================================
Proof complete.
```

### observedResult

All sensitive runtime context data (`<relevant-memories>`, `Sender (untrusted metadata)`, `Conversation info`, user `open_id`, context header) is completely removed from the simulated leaked reply. The actual user-visible reply text ("Here is my analysis..." / "proceed further") is preserved intact.

The fix adds 3 lines to production code (`INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_BEGIN`, the existing body, `INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_END`) and imports 2 additional symbols. The existing `stripInternalRuntimeContext` (which processes delimited blocks first) handles the rest without any changes to the stripping logic.

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `runtime-context-prompt.test.ts` — 21/21 (updated to expect delimiters)
- [x] `replay-history.test.ts` — passed
- [x] `sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts` — 142/142
- [x] `internal-runtime-context.test.ts` — 5/5
- [x] `stripInternalRuntimeContext` correctly removes delimited blocks (existing behavior, unchanged)
- [x] No changes to stripping logic — only the format of the built context changed

---

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*
target.author: zhangqueping
